varieties.name: Bodegold
varieties.origin: Germany, modern tetraploid (4x) variety, developed for intensive commercial production
varieties.traits: Large uniform flowers 22mm diameter, grows 60-77cm tall, early blooming (2-3 weeks earlier than standard), robust tetraploid growth
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - abundant flower production suitable for tea harvest, produces more flowers than diploid varieties
varieties.availability: Available through specialized herb seed suppliers; The Seed Collection (Australia), Renee's Garden, Johnny's Selected Seeds internationally

varieties.name: Zloty Lan
varieties.origin: Poland, tetraploid (4x) variety bred for commercial production, name translates to 'Golden Fields'
varieties.traits: Vigorous tetraploid plants 30-40cm tall, larger flowers than diploid varieties, strong erect growth habit, robust and more vigorous than Bodegold
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptionally high - produces more flowers than Bodegold, dry yields up to 1200kg/ha, blooms profusely June-July in temperate zones

varieties.name: Standard German Chamomile
varieties.origin: Native to Southern and Eastern Europe, naturalized worldwide, traditional open-pollinated form
varieties.traits: Annual growing 30-60cm tall, branching stems with finely divided ferny foliage, hollow conical flower centers (key identification feature)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high flower production, self-seeds freely, flowers suitable for tea and medicinal use

varieties.name: Bona
varieties.origin: Czech Republic/Slovakia, registered 1984, diploid (2x) variety developed through selection
varieties.traits: Diploid plants 70-74cm tall, more compact than tetraploid varieties, earlier flowering, stable production cycle
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good - lower yield than tetraploid varieties but consistent quality, suitable for tea and oil production

varieties.name: Goral (Kosice-II)
varieties.origin: Slovakia, tetraploid (4x) variety adapted for mechanical harvesting
varieties.traits: Vigorous tetraploid plants 85.5cm tall (tallest variety), uniform flowering horizon, strong stems suitable for mechanical harvest
varieties.yield_notes: Highest flower yield among tested varieties, excellent for commercial production, consistent quality across regions

varieties.name: Germania
varieties.traits: Diploid cultivar 74.2cm tall, stable production cycle, uniform growth, shorter than tetraploid varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate - primarily focused on dried flower quality rather than oil production, consistent flower formation

varieties.name: Roman Chamomile (Standard)
varieties.origin: Western Europe and North Africa, ancient heirloom variety cultivated since Egyptian times
varieties.traits: Low-growing perennial 10-30cm, spreading by rooting stems, feathery apple-scented foliage, curlier leaves than German chamomile, solid yellow flower centers
varieties.yield_notes: Lower flower yield than German chamomile but perennial growth provides ongoing harvest, flowers less prolifically but continuously
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Australian Plants Online, Brisbane Brugmansia Angel's Trumpets, Norfolk Herbs (UK ships globally)

varieties.name: Treneague (Lawn Chamomile)
varieties.origin: England, non-flowering cultivar selected for lawn/groundcover use, propagated vegetatively only
varieties.traits: Dense mat-forming perennial 5-10cm tall, very low growing, spreads by rooting stems, highly aromatic apple-scented foliage when crushed, non-flowering clone
varieties.yield_notes: N/A for flowers - grown for fragrant foliage groundcover, lawn alternative, or aromatic pathways
varieties.availability: Must buy as plants (not seeds) - Norfolk Herbs, Jekka's Herbetum (UK), Hetty's Herbs, Pennard Plants (UK), specialty herb nurseries. Limited Australian availability.

varieties.name: Vallary 🧬
varieties.origin: INDIA - developed by CIMAP (Central Institute of Medicinal & Aromatic Plants), Lucknow, released 1996
varieties.traits: Developed for subtropical plains of North India, improved yield over traditional types, bred for Indian soil and climate tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Enhanced yield compared to unimproved varieties, suitable for commercial production in appropriate regions
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, CIMAP Lucknow, agricultural research stations in Uttar Pradesh, may be difficult to source in Kerala

varieties.name: Prashant 🧬
varieties.origin: INDIA - developed by CIMAP Lucknow, newer variety bred for improved oil characteristics
varieties.traits: Bred specifically for North Indian conditions, improved over Vallary, better suited to Indian cultivation practices
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yield and better quality than Vallary, recommended variety for commercial cultivation in suitable regions of India
varieties.availability: CIMAP research center, Indian agricultural universities, limited commercial availability, may require contact with research institutions

varieties.name: Standard German Chamomile
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, introduced to India during Mughal period (17th century), grown in India 300+ years
varieties.traits: Annual 30-60cm, introduced medicinal herb traditionally grown around Lucknow for 300+ years, used in Unani system of medicine
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate in traditional cultivation, varies greatly with climate suitability
varieties.availability: AllThatGrows.in (Indian online supplier), Nurserylive India, Magic Farms (South India - provides chamomile from controlled cultivation), Indian herb nurseries
